page: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor architecture operating at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, but supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 463.87 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 200.05 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21904.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU metric of 99340.45. Database operations perform in the top tier, scoring 37055.11 in Passmark database tests, while Redis SET operations achieve 89.22 million operations per second.

Single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1692.0. Large-scale LLM inference presents a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model falling into the bottom 25% at 6.91 tokens per second. Given the high memory density, top-tier memory bandwidth, and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing, while being less optimal for heavy single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epsv6 family, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processors running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory capabilities, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 463.87 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 200.05 GB/sec. Multi-core execution is strong, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21904.0, whereas single-core metrics and large LLM inference prompt processing represent relative performance tradeoffs. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and 384 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ps_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 48 dedicated cores and 384 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring high memory density and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores operating at 3.4 GHz
384.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 463.87 GB/sec large-block read speed
Strong multi-core performance and database operation execution
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and multi-threaded processing
